text:
### [ F20 Enforcement of an order: requirement for a penal notice E+W


10.12. At the time when the order is drawn up, the court officer will—


issue a copy of the order, endorsed with or incorporating a notice as to the consequences of disobedience, for service in accordance with rule 10.6.


(For enforcement of an order by way of committal see Part 37 [ F21 (rule 37.4(2)(c) requires a contempt application to include confirmation that any order allegedly breached or disobeyed contained a penal notice) ] .)


Textual Amendments


F20 Rules 10.12, 10.13 substituted (22.4.2014) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2014 (S.I. 2014/667) , rules 1 , 15 (with rule 45 )


F21 Words in rule 10.12 substituted (1.10.2020) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/758) , rules 1(1) , 3(1)


### Enforcement of an undertaking E+W


10.13. F22 ... Part 37 applies F23 ... where an application is made to commit a person for breach of an undertaking.


[ F24 (Rule 37.4(2) includes requirements for the information to be contained in a contempt application in relation to breach of an undertaking.) ] .) ]


Textual Amendments


F20 Rules 10.12, 10.13 substituted (22.4.2014) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2014 (S.I. 2014/667) , rules 1 , 15 (with rule 45 )


F22 Words in rule 10.13 omitted (1.10.2020) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/758) , rules 1(1) , 3(2)(a)


F23 Words in rule 10.13 omitted (1.10.2020) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/758) , rules 1(1) , 3(2)(b)

metadata.title: Part 10 — Applications under Part 4 of the Family Law Act 1996
